seo.defaultTitle: Ergo Platform - Resilient Blockchain for Contractual Money
seo.defaultDescription: Ergo builds advanced cryptographic features and radically new DeFi functionality on the rock-solid foundations laid by a decade of blockchain theory and development.
seo.keywords: blockchain, cryptocurrency, DeFi, smart contracts, Ergo, UTXO, proof of work

technology.title: Technology
technology.subtitle: Advanced blockchain technology
technology.description: Explore Ergo's cutting-edge features: eUTXO, ErgoScript, Sigma Protocols, and more.

eutxoModel.title: eUTXO Model
eutxoModel.description: Extended UTXO model that enables parallel smart contract execution and composable DeFi applications. Eliminates reentrancy attacks while maintaining Bitcoin's proven security model.

parallelExecution.title: Parallel Execution
parallelExecution.description: Smart contracts run independently without global state conflicts.

composability.title: Composability
composability.description: Build complex DeFi applications from simple, reusable components.

ergoScript.title: ErgoScript
ergoScript.description: Powerful smart contract language designed for security and flexibility. Features built-in Sigma protocols for zero-knowledge proofs and formal verification support for bulletproof contracts.

formalVerification.title: Formal Verification
formalVerification.description: Mathematically prove your smart contracts are secure and bug-free.

builtInPrivacy.title: Built-in Privacy
builtInPrivacy.description: Native zero-knowledge proofs and ring signatures for confidential transactions.

safeByDesign.title: Safe by Design
safeByDesign.description: Prevents common vulnerabilities like reentrancy attacks and unexpected behaviors.

autolykos.title: Autolykos PoW
autolykos.description: Energy-efficient, ASIC-resistant mining algorithm that democratizes network participation. Designed for GPU mining to keep the network decentralized and accessible to everyone.

energyEfficient.title: Energy Efficient
energyEfficient.description: Memory-hard algorithm reduces power consumption compared to traditional PoW.

soloMining.title: Solo Mining Friendly
soloMining.description: Low barrier to entry enables individual miners to participate profitably.

asicResistant.title: ASIC Resistant
asicResistant.description: Optimized for consumer GPUs to prevent mining centralization.

storageRent.title: Storage Rent
storageRent.description: Revolutionary economic model that prevents blockchain bloat. Inactive coins pay minimal rent after 4 years, ensuring sustainable blockchain size and permanent miner rewards.

sustainableBlockchain.title: Sustainable Blockchain
sustainableBlockchain.description: Automatic cleanup of unused data keeps the blockchain lean and efficient.

perpetualSecurity.title: Perpetual Security
perpetualSecurity.description: Miners receive rewards forever, ensuring long-term network security.

resourceEfficient.title: Resource Efficient
resourceEfficient.description: Maintains lightweight blockchain that runs on modest hardware.

sigmaProtocols.title: Sigma Protocols
sigmaProtocols.description: Built-in zero-knowledge proofs enable privacy-preserving transactions and advanced cryptographic features without third-party solutions or complex setups.

optionalPrivacy.title: Optional Privacy
optionalPrivacy.description: Choose between transparent or confidential transactions as needed.

advancedSecurity.title: Advanced Security
advancedSecurity.description: Multi-signature wallets, threshold signatures, and secure voting systems.

developerFriendly.title: Developer Friendly
developerFriendly.description: Simple APIs to add privacy features to any application.

sections.ledgerModels: Blockchain Ledger Models: UTXO vs. Account
sections.keyDifferences: eUTXO vs. Account Model — Key Differences
sections.useCases: eUTXO in Action — When It Shines

utxo.title: UTXO Model (Bitcoin-style)
utxo.description: No global balances — only a collection of unspent outputs.
utxo.analogy: Analogy: Like having cash bills/coins. Each transaction spends some bills, creates new ones.
utxo.strengthsText: Simplicity, privacy, parallel processing, easier audit trails.
utxo.weaknessText: Not naturally suited for stateful dApps.

account.title: Account Model (Ethereum-style)
account.description: Global state: Each account has a balance and (for contracts) its own data storage.
account.analogy: Analogy: Like a bank account — all assets tracked in one place.
account.strengthsText: Intuitive, flexible for stateful apps, natural for Turing-complete contracts.
account.weaknessesText: Potential for re-entrancy bugs, unpredictable gas, global state bloat.

conclusion.text: The eUTXO model is a quantum leap: it keeps UTXO's clarity, security, and scalability while unlocking true programmability. For developers who want robust, auditable, and future-proof dApps — Ergo's approach is both powerful and elegant.

ergoScriptPage.title: ErgoScript
ergoScriptPage.subtitle: Smart Contract Language
ergoScriptPage.description: Powerful, secure smart contract language with built-in cryptographic primitives and formal verification support.

formalVerification.title: Formal Verification Support
formalVerification.description: Supports formal verification and auditability via declarative constraints and first-class crypto primitives

eutxoSecurity.title: eUTXO Security Model
eutxoSecurity.description: The class of reentrancy vulnerabilities common to account-based systems is absent in eUTXO

deterministicExecution.title: Deterministic Execution
deterministicExecution.description: Computational complexity is predictable, though network fees are market-driven

nativeSigmaProtocols.title: Native Sigma Protocols
nativeSigmaProtocols.description: First-class support for multi-signature, threshold, and ring-signature constructions

utxoConcurrency.title: UTXO Concurrency
utxoConcurrency.description: Parallel transaction processing with batcher patterns for high-contention scenarios

richDataTypes.title: Rich Data Types
richDataTypes.description: Native support for complex data structures, collections, and cryptographic operations

lightClients.title: Light Clients
lightClients.description: Secure verification on mobile or web without downloading the full blockchain.

fastVerification.title: Fast Verification
fastVerification.description: Verify chain work in seconds using compact proofs instead of full syncs.

trustless.title: Trustless
trustless.description: No trusted relays or validators; verification follows PoW assumptions.

mobileWallets.title: Mobile Wallets
mobileWallets.description: Light wallets that prove chain work with succinct proofs.

oracleSystems.title: Oracle Systems
oracleSystems.description: Efficient verification of external chain data.

sidechains.title: Sidechains
sidechains.description: Sidechains can verify L1 succinctly for security and liveness.

finalityHandling.nipopows: Observe window; new proof can supersede

eventOccurs.title: Event Occurs
eventOccurs.description: A transaction or block appears on Ergo.

proofGeneration.title: Proof Generation
proofGeneration.description: A succinct proof is built from sampled headers.

verification.title: Verification
verification.description: Anyone verifies the proof quickly and trustlessly.

privacyFeaturesPage.title: Privacy Features
privacyFeaturesPage.subtitle: Sigma Protocols & Confidential Transactions
privacyFeaturesPage.description: Advanced cryptographic privacy tools built into Ergo's protocol. Zero-knowledge proofs, ring signatures, and confidential transactions without trusted setups.

sigmaProtocols.title: Sigma Protocols
sigmaProtocols.description: Enable powerful privacy tools like ring signatures and zero-knowledge proofs at the protocol level.

ergoMixer.title: ErgoMixer
ergoMixer.description: On-chain, non-custodial mixing for transaction privacy and anonymity.

confidentialAssets.title: Confidential Assets
confidentialAssets.description: Issue tokens with hidden amounts and rules for maximum privacy.

advantages: No trusted setup needed — pure cryptographic security
Flexible privacy features usable in dApps, DeFi, and NFTs
Built into the protocol, not added as afterthought

privateTransactions.title: Private Transactions
privateTransactions.description: Mix your ERG and tokens to achieve strong on-chain anonymity (within the current anonymity set)

confidentialDefi.title: Confidential DeFi
confidentialDefi.description: Build DeFi applications with private balances and logic

anonymousVoting.title: Anonymous Voting
anonymousVoting.description: Create DAOs and voting systems with secret ballots

privateNfts.title: Private NFTs
privateNfts.description: NFTs with hidden metadata or ownership information

oraclePoolsPage.title: Oracle Pools
oraclePoolsPage.subtitle: Decentralized Price Feeds
oraclePoolsPage.description: Reliable, consensus-based oracle system providing accurate price feeds for DeFi applications without central authorities or single points of failure.

reliablePriceFeeds.title: Reliable Price Feeds
reliablePriceFeeds.description: Consensus-based data aggregation ensures accurate, manipulation-resistant prices

universalCompatibility.title: Universal Compatibility
universalCompatibility.description: Any smart contract can access oracle data without special integrations

fullyDecentralized.title: Fully Decentralized
fullyDecentralized.description: No central authority or single point of failure in the data pipeline

economicSecurity.title: Economic Security
economicSecurity.description: Stake-based incentives ensure honest data provision

consensusMechanism.title: Consensus Mechanism
consensusMechanism.description: Outlier detection and averaging for robust data quality

lowLatencyUpdates.title: Low Latency Updates
lowLatencyUpdates.description: Frequent updates ensure data freshness for DeFi applications

dataAggregation.title: Data Aggregation
dataAggregation.content: The oracle pool uses a robust averaging mechanism that filters out outliers by removing the top and bottom 25% of submissions, then calculating the average of remaining values for consensus.

updateTriggers.title: Update Triggers
updateTriggers.content: New data is published when price deviation exceeds threshold (e.g., 0.5%), maximum time since last update (e.g., 1 hour), or minimum oracle submissions are reached.

economicSecurity.title: Economic Security
economicSecurity.content: Oracle operators must stake collateral, with slashing for malicious behavior and rewards for honest participation, creating strong economic incentives for reliable data.

attackResistance.title: Attack Resistance
attackResistance.content: Sybil attack protection via staking, outlier filtering prevents manipulation, and decentralization prevents censorship of oracle data.

sigmaUsdStablecoin.title: SigmaUSD Stablecoin
sigmaUsdStablecoin.description: Algorithmic stablecoin using ERG/USD price feed for collateral calculations

spectrumDex.title: Spectrum DEX
spectrumDex.description: Decentralized exchange using price feeds for limit orders and liquidations

lendingProtocols.title: Lending Protocols
lendingProtocols.description: Collateralized lending using oracle prices for liquidation thresholds

subblocksPage.title: Subblocks
subblocksPage.subtitle: Sub-Second Confirmations
subblocksPage.description: Revolutionary scaling solution providing near-instant transaction finality with full Layer 1 security. No bridges, no compromises.

subSecondConfirmations.title: Sub-Second Confirmations
subSecondConfirmations.description: Near-instant transaction finality for improved user experience without sacrificing decentralization

layer1Security.title: Layer 1 Security
layer1Security.description: Full blockchain security without trusted intermediaries or bridges

seamlessIntegration.title: Seamless Integration
seamlessIntegration.description: Works with existing infrastructure - no bridges or sidechains needed

parallelProcessing.title: Parallel Processing
parallelProcessing.description: Multiple subblocks can be validated in parallel for increased throughput

adaptiveDifficulty.title: Adaptive Difficulty
adaptiveDifficulty.description: Dynamic adjustment ensures consistent block times even with varying hash power

minerIncentives.title: Miner Incentives
minerIncentives.description: Additional rewards for subblock miners while maintaining main chain security

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.content: Subblocks are intermediate blocks between main blocks that provide faster confirmations. They maintain the same security model as main blocks but with adjusted difficulty targets.

consensusMechanism.title: Consensus Mechanism
consensusMechanism.content: Uses a modified version of Autolykos that allows for variable difficulty targets. Subblocks contribute to the overall chain weight while maintaining compatibility with existing infrastructure.

transactionOrdering.title: Transaction Ordering
transactionOrdering.content: Transactions in subblocks are ordered deterministically using a combination of timestamp and transaction ID, preventing front-running and ensuring fairness.

stateManagement.title: State Management
stateManagement.content: Subblocks update the UTXO set incrementally, allowing for efficient state transitions without full block validation overhead.

defiApplications.title: DeFi Applications
defiApplications.description: Enable high-frequency trading and instant swaps with sub-second finality

gaming.title: Gaming
gaming.description: Real-time game state updates without waiting for block confirmations

socialApplications.title: Social Applications
socialApplications.description: Instant messaging and social interactions on-chain

velvetForksPage.title: Velvet Forks
velvetForksPage.subtitle: Smooth Protocol Evolution
velvetForksPage.description: Revolutionary upgrade mechanism enabling backward-compatible protocol improvements without forced upgrades or network splits.

backwardCompatible.title: Backward Compatible
backwardCompatible.description: No forced upgrades, no chain wars - new features coexist with non-upgraded nodes

futureProof.title: Future Proof
futureProof.description: Adopt innovations at your own pace without network disruption

gradualAdoption.title: Gradual Adoption
gradualAdoption.description: Evolutionary, not revolutionary changes - the network evolves smoothly

consensusPreservation.title: Consensus Preservation
consensusPreservation.description: Maintains network consensus even with different node versions

securityFirst.title: Security First
securityFirst.description: New features only activate when safe majority adoption is reached

developerFriendly.title: Developer Friendly
developerFriendly.description: Test new features on mainnet without breaking existing applications

nipopowsIntegration.title: NiPoPoWs Integration
nipopowsIntegration.description: Non-Interactive Proofs of Proof-of-Work can be added as an optional feature, enabling light clients without forcing all nodes to support them.

privacyEnhancements.title: Privacy Enhancements
privacyEnhancements.description: Advanced privacy features like ring signatures or stealth addresses can be introduced without breaking existing transparent transactions.

smartContractExtensions.title: Smart Contract Extensions
smartContractExtensions.description: New opcodes and scripting capabilities can be added for developers who want to use them, while existing contracts continue to work.

consensusImprovements.title: Consensus Improvements
consensusImprovements.description: Optimizations to block validation, mining algorithms, or network protocols can be tested and adopted gradually.

adaptiveEmissionPage.title: Adaptive Emission
adaptiveEmissionPage.subtitle: Flexible Economic Parameters
adaptiveEmissionPage.description: Community-driven governance system enabling parameter adjustments through miner voting, ensuring long-term sustainability and network adaptability.

parameterTuning.title: Parameter Tuning
parameterTuning.description: Economic variables can be adjusted based on network conditions

economicFlexibility.title: Economic Flexibility
economicFlexibility.description: Adapt to changing network needs without hard forks

communityInput.title: Community Input

minerVoting.title: Miner Voting
minerVoting.description: Miners vote on parameter changes through block headers

gradualChanges.title: Gradual Changes
gradualChanges.description: Parameters adjust smoothly to prevent economic shocks

longTermSustainability.title: Long-term Sustainability
longTermSustainability.description: Ensures network security even after emission ends

emissionSchedule.title: Emission Schedule
emissionSchedule.description: Declining emission with periodic reductions and post-emission sustainability through storage rent and transaction fees.

storageRentEconomics.title: Storage Rent Economics
storageRentEconomics.description: UTXOs unspent for 4+ years pay storage fees, providing sustainable miner revenue and preventing state bloat.

minerVotingProcess.title: Miner Voting Process
minerVotingProcess.description: Community proposals, 1024-epoch voting period, 90% threshold requirement, and gradual activation.

economicParameters.title: Economic Parameters
economicParameters.description: Adjustable parameters include block size, storage fee factor, min fee, and computational cost limits.

nativeAssets.description: First-class tokens with protocol-level security guarantees, not contract-based

richMetadata.description: Support for complex metadata including images, descriptions, and custom properties

universalCompatibility.title: Universal Compatibility
universalCompatibility.description: Any smart contract can access oracle data without special integrations

defiTokens.description: Create governance tokens, utility tokens, and reward tokens for DeFi protocols

nftCollections.description: Build unique digital art, collectibles, and gaming assets

stablecoins.title: Stablecoins
stablecoins.description: Issue algorithmic or collateralized stable value tokens

storageRentPage.subtitle: Sustainable Blockchain Economics
storageRentPage.description: Ergo's storage rent mechanism prevents blockchain bloat and ensures long-term sustainability by recycling unused outputs.

blockchainBloat.description: Traditional blockchains accumulate unused data over time, leading to inefficiency and higher costs.

forgottenWallets.description: Lost private keys mean funds are stuck forever, reducing the effective money supply.

scalabilityIssues.title: Scalability Issues
scalabilityIssues.description: Growing blockchain size makes it harder for new nodes to sync and participate in the network.

automaticCleanup.title: Automatic Cleanup
automaticCleanup.description: Storage rent automatically removes old, unused outputs from the blockchain state.

fundRecycling.title: Fund Recycling
fundRecycling.description: Coins from forgotten wallets are gradually returned to circulation through miners.

sustainableGrowth.title: Sustainable Growth
sustainableGrowth.description: Blockchain size remains manageable, ensuring long-term network health.

creation.title: Box Creation
creation.description: When you create a UTXO (box), it starts with full value and no rent due.

aging.title: Aging Process
aging.description: After 4 years of inactivity, the box becomes subject to storage rent fees.

collection.title: Rent Collection
collection.description: Miners can collect rent, gradually reducing the box value until it's spent or recycled.

recycling.title: Full Recycling
recycling.description: If rent isn't paid, the remaining value goes to miners and the box is removed.

eutxoModelPage.title: eUTXO Model
eutxoModelPage.subtitle: Extended UTXO for Smart Contracts
eutxoModelPage.description: Ergo's eUTXO model combines Bitcoin's security with Ethereum's programmability, enabling parallel processing and predictable fees.

stateManagement.utxo: No global balances — only unspent outputs
stateManagement.account: Global state with account balances and storage

security.utxo: The reentrancy vulnerability class common to account-based systems is absent in eUTXO; script execution cost is bounded
security.account: Re-entrancy class typical for account-based systems; gas costs can be unpredictable

privacy.utxo: UTXO mixing, Σ-protocols for advanced privacy
privacy.account: Global state makes privacy harder by default

tokenHandling.utxo: Tokens are first-class citizens in boxes

faq.title: Need Help?
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about Ergo, its technology, and the ecosystem. If you can't find what you're looking for, join our community channels.
faq.noResults: No questions found. Try a different search or filter.

whatIsErgo.q: What is Ergo in simple terms?
whatIsErgo.a: Ergo is a secure Proof-of-Work blockchain for powerful smart contracts, enabling decentralized financial applications. Think of it as digital money (ERG) with programmable rules.

isErgoCompany.a: No, Ergo is a decentralized, open-source protocol. The Ergo Foundation is a non-profit community body that supports development but does not control the network.

fairLaunch.a: Ergo had no Initial Coin Offering (ICO) or pre-mined tokens for founders. This ensures a more equitable distribution, as everyone had an equal opportunity to acquire ERG through mining from day one.

ergoDifference.q: How is Ergo different from Bitcoin or Ethereum?
ergoDifference.a: vs. Bitcoin: Ergo supports advanced smart contracts, not just currency. \n vs. Ethereum: Ergo uses the eUTXO model for enhanced security and predictable fees, and remains Proof-of-Work.

whatIsEutxo.a: The Extended UTXO model attaches data and logic directly to "boxes" of money. This enhances smart contract security, fee predictability, and allows for better scalability compared to the account model.

whatIsErgoscript.a: ErgoScript is the powerful and secure programming language for writing smart contracts on Ergo. You do not need to know code to use Ergo; it's a tool for developers.

sigmaProtocols.a: These are advanced zero-knowledge proofs built into Ergo's core, enabling privacy features like ErgoMixer, complex multi-party contracts, and secure multi-signature schemes.

ergCoinUse.q: What is the ERG coin used for?
ergCoinUse.a: ERG is used for transaction fees, collateral for stablecoins (SigmaUSD), participating in DeFi applications, powering dApps (NFTs, gaming), and incentivizing miners.

howToGetErg.a: You can acquire ERG on various cryptocurrency exchanges, through peer-to-peer trades, or by mining. Always do your own research before investing.

bestWallet.q: Which wallet is best for beginners?
bestWallet.a: The official mobile wallets (Android/iOS) or the Nautilus Wallet (browser extension) are excellent, user-friendly starting points.

dappsOnErgo.q: What kinds of dApps are on Ergo?
dappsOnErgo.a: Ergo's ecosystem includes DeFi platforms (Spectrum, Duckpools), privacy tools (ErgoMixer), NFT marketplaces (SkyHarbor), cross-chain bridges (Rosen), and more.

ergoMixer.a: ErgoMixer is a non-custodial service that increases your transaction privacy by mixing your ERG with others, making it difficult to trace your transaction history.

whoGoverns.a: Ergo is decentralized and community-driven. Protocol changes are discussed and formalized through Ergo Improvement Proposals (EIPs), with miners signaling support.

howToContribute.q: How can I contribute if I'm not a developer?
howToContribute.a: You can help with community support, content creation (articles, videos), translation, testing dApps, or simply spreading awareness about Ergo.
